full_prompt: Design the chassis and excavation-arm mount of a regolith mining robot benchmark, compliant with a 2026 guidebook draft. Robot stowed envelope must fit within 1.5 m length x 0.75 m width x 0.75 m height at the start of the mission; total robot mass must not exceed 80 kg. Operational arena is a 7.38 m x 3.88 m lunar-analog regolith bin filled with BP-1 simulant (bulk density ~1500 kg/m^3, angle of internal friction 45 deg). Robot must autonomously traverse the arena, excavate >= 15 kg of BP-1 in a 15-minute mission, and deposit it into a collection bin. Chassis material: 6061-T6 aluminum extrusion + plate (Young's modulus 68.9 GPa, Poisson 0.33, yield 276 MPa, ultimate 310 MPa, density 2700 kg/m^3). Excavation arm mount is the bolted interface plate where the active digging mechanism attaches; it must transmit the BP-1 digging reaction load into the chassis without bending the chassis out of kinematic alignment. Load cases: (LC1) BP-1 digging reaction: 500 N horizontal opposing motion + 300 N vertical downforce at the excavation arm tool tip, moment arm 0.4 m from arm-mount bolt pattern - total 680 N at mount with 260 N-m moment; (LC2) 15-minute duty cycle: 100 digging strokes per mission at 1 per 9 s, alternating loading between 0 and full LC1 at 0.11 Hz, 100 cycles; (LC3) traverse impact: 2 g vertical on one wheel crossing a BP-1 berm; (LC4) regolith-laden return: 15 kg BP-1 mass distributed on deposit hopper plus 80 kg robot at 1 g; (LC5) tip/tilt: 15 deg side slope with full regolith load; (LC6) dust-seal test: 5 kPa differential across bearing seals with BP-1 particulate loading. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises in chassis and mount <= 184 MPa (yield 276 / 1.5) across LC1-LC5; (R2) arm-mount bolt pattern distortion under LC1 <= 0.3 mm to preserve excavation kinematic accuracy; (R3) under LC2 fatigue, alternating stress at mount fillets <= 55 MPa (aluminum Kt=3 endurance at 10^5 cycles scaled by Miner); (R4) total robot mass <= 80 kg; (R5) stowed envelope compliance 1.5 x 0.75 x 0.75 m verified geometrically; (R6) first chassis torsional mode above 15 Hz. Deliverables: STEP, FEA per case, fatigue, modal, mass and envelope report.
short_prompt:
Design the chassis and excavation-arm mount of a regolith mining robot operating in a 7.38 x 3.88 m BP-1 simulant bin (bulk density ~1500 kg/m^3, phi ~45 deg), autonomously excavating >= 15 kg of BP-1 in a 15-minute mission. Material class: 6061-T6 aluminum extrusion + plate. No external structural standard is named; design to a 1.5 FoS on yield, with aluminum Kt=3 fatigue scaling at the arm-mount fillets.

Envelope: stowed 1.5 x 0.75 x 0.75 m, total robot mass <= 80 kg.

Load cases.
- LC1 BP-1 digging: 500 N horizontal opposing motion + 300 N down at the tool tip, 0.4 m arm giving 680 N at the mount + 260 N-m moment.
- LC2 duty cycle: 100 digging strokes per mission at ~0.11 Hz, alternating between 0 and full LC1.
- LC3 berm traverse: 2 g vertical on one wheel crossing a BP-1 berm.
- LC4 regolith-laden return: 80 kg robot + 15 kg BP-1 on the deposit hopper at 1 g.
- LC5 side slope: 15 deg (0.26 g lateral) with full regolith load.
- LC6 dust-seal: 5 kPa differential across bearing seals with BP-1 loading.

prompt.title: Regolith mining robot chassis + excavation arm mount
prompt.brief: 80 kg, 1.5 x 0.75 x 0.75 m stowed BP-1 mining robot with 500 N digging reaction.
